mission.title: falco: Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read more than 30MB of data
mission.description: Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read more than 30MB of data. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
**What happened**:
We observed an error log under a large scale load test.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read…
steps.description:
Looks like this is a "soft limit" that comes from Sysdig itself; and it was already risen in the past (https://github.com/draios/sysdig/pull/892/files).

 Should we open an issue there? Do you think it's something that can be somehow changed to be set dynamically? Or "gigantic k8s environments" are
```yaml
jq .system_info" -->
{
  "machine": "x86_64",
  "nodename": "falco-hgq5k",
  "release": "4.15.0-1034-gke",
  "sysname": "Linux",
  "version": "#36-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 6 11:16:45 UTC 2019"
}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Looks like this is a "soft limit" that comes from Sysdig itself; and it was already risen in the past (https://github.com/draios/sysdig/pull/892/files).
codeSnippets: Linux falco-hgq5k 4.15.0-1034-gke #36-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 6 11:16:45 UTC 2019 x86_64 GNU/Linux

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
